texts.label: text
texts.orig: In this course, students will develop problem solving skills, critical thinking, computational proficiency, and contextual fluency through the study of equations, functions, and their graphs. Emphasis will be placed on quadratic, exponential, and logarithmic functions. Topics will include solving equations and inequalities, definition and properties of a function, domain and range, transformations of graphs, operations on functions, composite and inverse functions, basic polynomial and rational functions, exponential and logarithmic functions, and applications. Student learning outcomes: students will solve an equation or an inequality using an appropriate technique; students will define and describe functions, their properties, and graphs; students will manipulate functions to simplify expressions and find new functions; students will use transformations to write an equation for a function and to graph a function; and students will model and solve real world problems using functions. Prerequisite: MAT 1033 with a grade of 'C' or better or satisfactory score/grade on an approved placement method. Computational Course.
texts.text: In this course, students will develop problem solving skills, critical thinking, computational proficiency, and contextual fluency through the study of equations, functions, and their graphs. Emphasis will be placed on quadratic, exponential, and logarithmic functions. Topics will include solving equations and inequalities, definition and properties of a function, domain and range, transformations of graphs, operations on functions, composite and inverse functions, basic polynomial and rational functions, exponential and logarithmic functions, and applications. Student learning outcomes: students will solve an equation or an inequality using an appropriate technique; students will define and describe functions, their properties, and graphs; students will manipulate functions to simplify expressions and find new functions; students will use transformations to write an equation for a function and to graph a function; and students will model and solve real world problems using functions. Prerequisite: MAT 1033 with a grade of 'C' or better or satisfactory score/grade on an approved placement method. Computational Course.

texts.orig: To successfully complete this course, you need to spend at least an average of 5 hours per week on the course, which includes readings, postings, tests, exams, etc. Set up a weekly time schedule that allows you sufficient time to complete the assigned course work by the required due dates. Plan to check-in and complete assignments at least three times a week .

Plan to check-in and complete assignments at least three times a week .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: An Incomplete is given only where extenuating circumstances exist, such as documented medical problems or a death in the family, and is issued solely at the discretion of the instructor. If the instructor agrees to grant an Incomplete, a written agreement must be completed between the instructor and the student, specifying the coursework to be completed, in what manner, and by when. Failure to fulfill the terms of the contract by the end of the next major term will result in an "F" for the course. A student may not remove an Incomplete by registering in a subsequent term to re-take the course. For more information on Incomplete grades, please refer to the Students' Rights and Responsibilities .

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: A Comprehensive Center for Exceptional Students' Services (ACCESS) Services for Students With Disabilities
texts.text: A Comprehensive Center for Exceptional Students' Services (ACCESS) Services for Students With Disabilities

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Miami Dade College provides support services and accommodations to assist students with documented disabilities achieve their education goals. You may learn about these services and eligibility by contacting your campus's ACCESS office. No retroactive accommodations will be provided.
texts.text: Miami Dade College provides support services and accommodations to assist students with documented disabilities achieve their education goals. You may learn about these services and eligibility by contacting your campus's ACCESS office. No retroactive accommodations will be provided.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For more information, please visit Miami Dade College Accessibility Statement, procedure 4055.
texts.text: For more information, please visit Miami Dade College Accessibility Statement, procedure 4055.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: There will be a distance learning fee assessed and possibly remote proctoring fees for this course. Please see Miami Dade's Tuition & Fees page for more information.
texts.text: There will be a distance learning fee assessed and possibly remote proctoring fees for this course. Please see Miami Dade's Tuition & Fees page for more information.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Hurricane and Other Natural Disasters: In the event of a hurricane or other disaster, the class follows the schedule established by the College for campus-based courses. Please visit the MDC website or call the MDC Hotline (305-237-7500) for situation updates.
texts.text: Hurricane and Other Natural Disasters: In the event of a hurricane or other disaster, the class follows the schedule established by the College for campus-based courses. Please visit the MDC website or call the MDC Hotline (305-237-7500) for situation updates.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: AI tools, including ChatGPT and Copilot, can be valuable resources for learning, brainstorming, and gaining a better understanding of course materials. However, these tools should not be used to complete in-class assignments or discussion tasks unless your instructor or the assignment specifically permits it. Assignments will be checked for AI-generated content. If an AI assessment
texts.text: AI tools, including ChatGPT and Copilot, can be valuable resources for learning, brainstorming, and gaining a better understanding of course materials. However, these tools should not be used to complete in-class assignments or discussion tasks unless your instructor or the assignment specifically permits it. Assignments will be checked for AI-generated content. If an AI assessment

texts.label: text
texts.orig: suggests possible unauthorized use or negative results, the faculty member may decide to take appropriate further action.
texts.text: suggests possible unauthorized use or negative results, the faculty member may decide to take appropriate further action.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Honorlock is an online exam proctoring service that helps maintain academic integrity during remote exams. Honorlock operates 24/7 without requiring appointments. To use Honorlock, MDC Online students need a stable and reliable internet connection, a computer with the Google Chrome browser and the Honorlock extension installed, a webcam, a microphone, and a valid photo ID.
texts.text: Honorlock is an online exam proctoring service that helps maintain academic integrity during remote exams. Honorlock operates 24/7 without requiring appointments. To use Honorlock, MDC Online students need a stable and reliable internet connection, a computer with the Google Chrome browser and the Honorlock extension installed, a webcam, a microphone, and a valid photo ID.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Honorlock monitors exam sessions using AI and live U.S.-based proctors. If issues are detected, a proctor intervenes via chat. Exams are accessed through Canvas.
texts.text: Honorlock monitors exam sessions using AI and live U.S.-based proctors. If issues are detected, a proctor intervenes via chat. Exams are accessed through Canvas.

table_cells.text: Valid photo ID. Google Chrome with the Honorlock extension. Webcam and microphone. Conduct a mandatory 360-degree room scan. Ensure proper lighting and remain fully visible on camera. No headphones, phones, notes, or other devices are allowed. No breaks are permitted once the exam begins. A quiet, private environment is required (no beds/sofas).

All exams must be taken independently. Accessing course content, notes, or other materials is prohibited . Violations (e.g., missing room scan, suspicious behavior, using unauthorized materials) will result in a zero on the exam and up to course failure . All sessions are recorded and flagged activity will be reviewed by your instructor.

grid.text: Valid photo ID. Google Chrome with the Honorlock extension. Webcam and microphone. Conduct a mandatory 360-degree room scan. Ensure proper lighting and remain fully visible on camera. No headphones, phones, notes, or other devices are allowed. No breaks are permitted once the exam begins. A quiet, private environment is required (no beds/sofas).

All exams must be taken independently. Accessing course content, notes, or other materials is prohibited . Violations (e.g., missing room scan, suspicious behavior, using unauthorized materials) will result in a zero on the exam and up to course failure . All sessions are recorded and flagged activity will be reviewed by your instructor.

table_cells.text: Honorlock offers 24/7 live support. You can find additional resources and support by visiting the MDC Online Proctored Exam Hub.
No makeups or exceptions for missed exams. Ensure all personal and technical preparations are completed in advance. Clear your calendar for remote exams.
